title: Macquarie Investment Management Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court declared that Macquarie Investment Management Limited (MIML), as superannuation platform trustee, contravened the Corporations Act by failing to place the Shield Master Fund on a watch list for heightened monitoring despite warning signs, after more than $480 million was invested in Shield by at least 5,800 consumers (mostly via superannuation platforms) since February 2022. Macquarie separately admitted contraventions and, under a court-enforceable undertaking, paid approximately $321 million to over 3,000 affected members — 100% of the amounts they invested less any withdrawals.
scope: Federal Court declared contraventions of the Corporations Act for failing to place a fund on a watch list for heightened monitoring despite warning signs; Paid approximately $321 million to over 3,000 affected members under a court-enforceable undertaking, following admitted contraventions; Fund at issue attracted more than $480 million from at least 5,800 consumers since February 2022 before the failures were addressed
